brief: The highest-margin opportunities are heavily concentrated in Brooklyn market-rate/overlay deals (specifically R7A and R7-2 zones in Crown Heights, Fort Greene, and Williamsburg/Bed-Stuy clusters) where combining 485-x with Section 8 VPS rents yields 12.4%-13.7% cash-on-cash returns and nearly $1M in land value headroom per site. Conversely, the Bronx program sites (ELLA/LIHTC) show negative land headroom against comp estimates ($531k est. land vs $196k walk-away), meaning they only pencil if acquired at nominal cost through city disposition with full subsidy awards. Overall, 920 of 921 qualified lots pencil, but priority should be given to public RFPs for the high-performing Brooklyn clusters.

targets.play: Develop a 12-unit residential project using the 485-x tax exemption paired with Section 8 VPS rents to achieve a 13.7% cash-on-cash return.
targets.negotiation: Estimated land value is $591,738 against a walk-away threshold of $1,588,048, providing $996,310 in valuation headroom.
targets.risk: Navigating the HPD disposition process for city-owned land and verifying site conditions/zoning for R7A.

targets.play: Construct a 10-unit infill building capitalizing on 485-x and Section 8 VPS rents for a 12.7% cash-on-cash yield.
targets.negotiation: Estimated land value is $569,141 versus a $1,525,802 walk-away price, yielding $956,661 in headroom.
targets.risk: HPD disposition process timelines and site condition diligence on an R7-2 parcel.

targets.play: Execute a 14-unit development via 485-x and Section 8 VPS rents as an anchor for a multi-lot Gerry/Bartlett Street assemblage, generating a 12.4% cash-on-cash return.
targets.negotiation: Estimated land value is $690,612 against a walk-away land price of $1,760,767, creating $1,070,155 in headroom.
targets.risk: HPD disposition process for public land and verifying assemblage zoning parameters in R7A.

targets.play: Deliver a 14-unit Queens R6A project utilizing 485-x tax benefits and Section 8 VPS rents targeting an 11.2% cash-on-cash return.
targets.negotiation: Estimated land cost of $622,014 against a $1,531,605 walk-away limit, offering $909,591 in headroom.
targets.risk: Zoning verification and site condition checks on coastal Queens acreage during HPD disposition.

targets.play: Sponsor an 8-unit all-affordable development utilizing an ELLA / 4% LIHTC capital stack to generate $280,948 in developer fee.
targets.negotiation: Estimated land comp cost of $531,360 far exceeds the $196,360 walk-away land ceiling, requiring discounted city disposition.
targets.risk: High subsidy competitiveness for program awards and reliance on HPD public disposition.

process_note: Next steps require verifying zoning and site conditions while monitoring HPD/EDC disposition pipelines to bid on the priority Brooklyn city-owned clusters.
disclaimer: AI interpretation of the engine's real screening output. Estimates pending diligence — not investment advice.
